w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: Cardholder entered the wrong PIN. May indicate forgotten PIN, keypad entry error, or possible unauthorized usage attempt.
Generic issuer decline; could be triggered by suspected fraud, insufficient balance, temporary issuer restrictions, or incorrect card data.
The issuer has disabled this type of transaction on the card (e.g., ATM withdrawals disabled, international restrictions, MCC/block settings).
Transaction flagged by issuer’s fraud monitoring system due to location/
mismatch, high-risk withdrawal pattern, or velocity checks.
The cardholder's account balance is not sufficient to dispense the requested withdrawal amount.
The issuer does not support the requested service or withdrawal type (e.g., account type not enabled for ATM withdrawals).
Issuer has restricted the transaction due to card usage policies or cardholder restrictions.
Withdrawal request exceeds the cardholder’s daily or per-transaction cash withdrawal limit.
The ATM is not configured or authorized to perform the requested transaction for the given card type/account type.
The card carries issuer-imposed usage restrictions (e.g., temporarily blocked, geographic restrictions, card control features).
The cardholder has manually blocked the card (e.g., via mobile banking) due to loss/theft or security concerns.
The selected account type does not exist for the card (e.g., customer selected Savings but only has Current/Credit account).
The requested operation is not valid for the card/account or ATM configuration (e.g., trying to withdraw from a non-cash account type).

The issuer’s system or intermediary switch was unavailable during processing. Additionally, ongoing Mastercard direct connectivity configuration is causing intermittent routing failures. Since the direct link could not be completed due to HSM issues, transactions are currently routed back via HBL. This fallback routing is unstable, resulting in frequent route errors.
General technical issue — may be due to network interruptions, timeouts, or temporary host-side processing failures.
The transaction message did not conform to expected ISO 8583 format; likely caused by incorrect field mapping or configuration mismatch.
